features.summary: Costco reported strong Q1 FY2025 results with net income of $1.798 billion ($4.04 per share) versus $1.589 billion ($3.58 per share) prior year. Net sales grew 7.5% to $60.99 billion with comparable sales up 5.2% (7.1% adjusted for gas deflation and FX). Traffic increased 5.1% worldwide while average ticket was up 0.1% (2% adjusted). The company opened 7 new warehouses in Q1 and projects 29 openings for fiscal 2025. Membership fee income grew 7.8% to $1.166 billion, with 77.4 million paid household members (up 7.6%) and renewal rates of 92.8% in US/Canada.
features.management_outlook: Management projects 29 warehouse openings in fiscal 2025 with 26 net new buildings, maintaining their expansion pace of approximately 30 warehouses annually over the next few years. They estimate full-year CapEx of approximately $5 billion, continuing gradual increases to support growth and technology investments. The company sees retail media as a significant future growth opportunity, though still in early stages, and expects continued strong performance in e-commerce and ancillary businesses. Management remains confident in their ability to deliver value to members through price investments, product innovation, and service improvements.
features.risks: Renewal rates face headwinds from higher growth in digital sign-ups which renew at slightly lower rates than the base, likely impacting published renewal rates through 2025. Supply chain challenges persist with predictability of on-time shipping remaining below pre-COVID levels and potential port strikes in India, East Coast, and Canada being monitored. Tariff uncertainty creates cost pressures, though management believes their merchants are well-equipped to navigate through alternative sourcing, inventory management, and vendor negotiations. Gas margin volatility continues to create quarterly unpredictability in gross margins.
features.key_drivers: Strong traffic growth of 5.1% worldwide driven by successful new warehouse openings, with the Pleasanton location achieving record opening day sales of $2.9 million. E-commerce growth of 13% supported by improved traffic, conversion rates, and average order values, with Costco Logistics completing nearly 1 million deliveries in Q1. Non-food categories showed particular strength with double-digit growth in gold/jewelry, gift cards, home furnishings, and sporting goods, while fresh foods led with high single-digit comps. Kirkland Signature continues growing faster than the overall business, reaching 33% penetration in US food and sundries.
